prompt_queue.prompt: Bootstrap the repository's non-technical, user-facing living documentation from source code. Treat this as a first-run documentation bootstrap. Study README.md, docs/**, source files, UI strings, commands, configuration, tests, examples, issues, and pull requests, then create a clean documentation surface that covers every major user-visible feature, setup path, configuration option, permission or requirement, common task, operational expectation, compatibility boundary, troubleshooting path, and FAQ-worthy behavior. Organize the output into a concise docs index plus separate digestible task/topic pages whose hierarchy mirrors the product's user-facing architecture, feature areas, and natural task flows. Use clear titles, bounded scope, parent/child relationships, and useful cross-links so readers can move from overview to focused instructions without reading a monolithic page. Explain implementation details when they affect user behavior. Prefer documentation that readers can navigate and understand quickly over audit artifacts. Use create_or_update_github_file before create_github_pull_request. If tool, token, or writable-scope limits constrain the pass, state the partial coverage in the committed docs and pull request body. Use no_changes when existing docs already provide a complete, navigable user docs surface across all user-visible behavior.
